text: Imagery practice improves endurance performance, with consistent support across experimental studies.
population: endurance athletes and physically active adults

text: Self-talk improves endurance performance, with consistent support across experimental studies.
population: endurance athletes and physically active adults

text: Goal setting improves endurance performance, with consistent support across experimental studies.
population: endurance athletes and physically active adults

text: Mental fatigue undermines endurance performance, suggesting athletes should minimize cognitive load before key sessions and races.
population: endurance athletes and physically active adults

text: Verbal encouragement during endurance exercise can improve performance.
population: endurance athletes and physically active adults

text: Head-to-head competition can improve endurance performance compared to exercising alone.
population: endurance athletes and physically active adults

text: Interventions that alter perception of effort consistently affect endurance performance, indicating that perceived effort is a key mediator of endurance output.
population: endurance athletes and physically active adults

text: It is unclear whether combining multiple psychological skills (e.g. imagery, self-talk, goal setting together) produces greater endurance performance benefits than training a single psychological skill.
population: endurance athletes and physically active adults
